message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="scott-wlaschin" color="purple" summary="Confirming domain implications from user stories">
Marty, excellent work on the user stories. I've read through all of them carefully and they're well-defined.

I want to confirm two domain model implications I found:

1. **Completion is reversible (US-6)**: "Completion can be undone (mark as pending again) -- toggling state." This means the TodoItem state machine is bidirectional: Pending <-> Completed. I've updated the glossary to include `uncomplete_todo()` as a domain action. This is good -- it doesn't add complexity to the type model (the enum stays the same), it just adds a reverse transition.

2. **Title max length is 200 characters (US-5)**: "Title has a maximum length (TBD by team -- suggested 200 characters)." I'll use 200 as the default and the team can adjust in the TDD cycle if needed.

3. **Emails are case-insensitive (US-2)**: "Case sensitivity in email (emails should be case-insensitive)." This means `ValidatedEmail::parse()` should normalize to lowercase. Good catch -- I'll add this to the glossary.

4. **Password minimum 8 characters (US-1)**: This is a boundary validation rule, not a domain type. The domain never sees raw passwords -- Argon2 hashing happens at the service/infrastructure boundary. The domain only cares about the hashed result.

These user stories are exactly the right level of detail. The acceptance criteria map cleanly to test cases for our TDD cycle.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Scott confirmed he's aligned on the domain model. But he mentioned 200 characters for the title max -- I already updated that to 300 in our earlier conversation and in the user stories. Let me make sure he got that message and isn't working from stale info.

input.content:
Scott, glad the stories work well for the domain model. Three quick things:

**1. Completion toggle -- confirmed.** You and I are aligned. Bidirectional `Pending <-> Completed` is correct per US-6. The enum stays clean.

**2. Title max length -- it's 300, not 200.** We discussed this in my earlier message to you. You proposed 300, I agreed, and I've already updated US-5 to say "300 characters (agreed with domain architect)." The committed version of `docs/user-stories.md` reflects this. Please update the glossary from 200 to 300 to match -- I noticed it still says `max 200 chars (per US-5)`.

**3. Email case-insensitive -- yes.** Normalize to lowercase in `ValidatedEmail::parse()`. Good.

**4. Password at the boundary -- agreed.** The domain never touches raw passwords. Argon2 hashing is infrastructure/service layer. The domain types don't need a `Password` type -- that lives at the boundary.

Everything else looks good. The domain model maps cleanly to the user stories.
input.summary: Title is 300 not 200, please update glossary

routing.summary: Title is 300 not 200, please update glossary

content.text: Corrected Scott on the title max length (300, not 200) and asked him to update the glossary. All four domain implications he raised are confirmed and aligned with the user stories. Waiting on the task breakdown discussion and the mob to start on registration.
